steps.title: Close the appositive
steps.content: The book title is followed by an em dash that introduces an appositive phrase: a combination of fiction and memoir, poetry and prose. That appositive must be closed before the main verb serves.

steps.title: Use the matching em dash
steps.content: A second em dash after prose closes the appositive and lets the main sentence continue. Choice A uses a semicolon, Choice B leaves the appositive unclosed, and Choice C uses a comma instead of the matching dash. The answer is D.
